title: Torularia glandulosa

This family consists of over 1,000 species, many of which are found in the temperate and subtropical regions of the world. The genus Torularia is a small group of plants that are native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. These plants are known for their small, blue flowers and their ability to thrive in dry environments. Within the Boraginaceae family, Torularia glandulosa is often grouped with other similar species, such as the torringtonia and the Cryptantha.
